note: Sprint 5.21: Task Builder From Scientific Intake v0.1. scripts/trinity/useful_compute_task_builder.py gains --from-scientific-intake <path> / --intake-task-kind {benchmark|comparison|extraction|validation} / --intake-output-schema. Loads a Sprint 5.20 TRINITY_SCIENTIFIC_PROMPT_INTAKE_<id>.json artifact, validates schema and safety_status (local_only / no_network / no_llm_call / deterministic_output all true), and builds a trinity-useful-compute-request/v0.1 manifest whose input_bundle_sha256 equals intake.combined_context_sha256 and whose metadata.scientific_intake records intake_id / combined_context_sha256 / prompt_sha256 / documents_count / intake_task_kind / intake_artifact_sha256. Schema is additive only: $id stays at trinity-useful-compute-request/v0.1, the const schema string is unchanged, new enum values 'trinity_scientific_prompt_intake' (source_tool) and 'scientific_intake' (task_type), new optional metadata.scientific_intake object — downstream worker / replay / governance / budget / proposal / draft remain compatible. The bridge does NOT copy document content into the request, does NOT store absolute paths, does NOT call LLM or network. In intake mode the legacy --source-tool / --input-bundle / --expected-output-schema / --public-description flags are rejected because their values are derived from the intake. Tests: 11 static-safety on the bridge (no subprocess / no network imports / no LLM tokens / no sost-cli / no wallet / no signing / no eval-exec / required intake safety flags listed / no document content propagation / no absolute path propagation), 26 functional (happy path, determinism, intake_task_kind changes request_id, safety-flag parametrised gates, wrong schema rejected, missing intake rejected, mutually-exclusive legacy flags rejected, schema validation, legacy --input-bundle path regression). Web: new card in trinity-useful-compute.html. No src/, no consensus, no RPC, no wallet, no signer, no wire-format change.
